_note: Vision-tier output. Produced by a multimodal model from rendered sheet images. Schema matches what extract_drawings.py requests from the Azure OpenAI backend, so these can be injected via --captions-file for offline/repeatable runs.
model: agent-vision (Claude, acting as the multimodal captioner)

captions.summary: Cover sheet for the 'Peters Creek Bridge' set. Contains a colour site photograph of the existing single-span steel through-truss highway bridge over Peters Creek on the Old Glenn Hwy, Chugiak, Alaska, plus the general notes, sheet index and title block.
Single 3D isometric of the complete bridge superstructure: a steel panelised truss with a transverse floor system. Members and panel-point joints A-F are leadered and labelled.

Plan, profile (side elevation) and transverse section of the bridge. Plan shows the panelised layout with three 16'-9" bays (symmetric) and overall deck widths of 27'-2"/25'-4", joints numbered 1-7, and callouts to the connection-detail sheets. The section shows the concrete deck carried on the truss with vertical/horizontal clearances.

Connection details for Joint A, the truss end-bearing panel point at the foundation. Two isometrics plus section and front views show the built-up top chord (twin channels laced with lattice plates and a cover plate), gusset plates, double connection angles, a W-section vertical, the end girder and deck beam, all seated on a foundation pin (pinned bearing).

Connection details for Joint B, the girder-to-truss floor connection. Includes an alternate girder connection drawn as a bolted double-angle shear connection on a 24"-deep girder with a 3/8" fillet weld both sides. Isometrics, top view, section and front view show the girder framing to the vertical through connection angles and gusset plates, with the concrete deck above.

Connection details for Joint C, a bottom-chord panel point where two diagonals, a vertical and the bottom chord meet on a bolted gusset plate; girder and deck beams also frame in. The front view gives bolt gauge and edge-distance dimensions for the gusset.

Connection details for Joints D and E, upper-chord panel points. Joint D shows the top chord, a diagonal and a vertical meeting on a bolted gusset plate (isometric + detail, referencing the built-up chord section 2/S3). Joint E shows the top chord passing over a vertical, plus the built-up twin-channel chord section laced with lattice plates.

Connection details for Joint F, an upper-chord panel point joining the top chord, a vertical and two diagonals on a bolted gusset plate (isometric + detail). Also a beam-beam connection detail showing a deck beam framed to the girder with double connection angles beneath the concrete slab.
